concerns.text: The plan assumes Llama-3-8B or Mistral-7B can run inference on a GitHub Actions free-tier runner (2 CPU, ~7 GB RAM) within 6 hours. Loading an 8B parameter model in float32 requires ~16 GB of RAM just for weights, exceeding the 7 GB constraint. Even with float16 (~8 GB), it leaves no room for the OS, Python runtime, or static analysis tools. This compute assumption is infeasible; the plan must switch to a smaller model (e.g., TinyLlama-1.1B) or acknowledge the need for a paid GPU runner.

The plan cites 'CodeQL' and 'tree-sitter' for static analysis but does not account for the memory overhead of running CodeQL on a 2 CPU/7 GB RAM runner. CodeQL is memory-intensive and may OOM on the free tier when analyzing large repositories, conflicting with the 'strict data hygiene' and 'no GPU' constraints without a specific memory-mitigation strategy (e.g., per-file analysis limits).
